let we now take a view of our present age . in all Asia , from whence Tully say , in epist . 1. ad q. the more simple , the more common ; the more common , the more natu•all ; the more natural , the more divine . but because ambition be a most fiery affection , and carry man blindfold into headlong hope , whereby many do aspire to bear rule , neither they good , nor with any good either mean or end ; the custom or law of nation have by two reins endeavour to keep in this rage desire : by succession and by election . and yet again , because election be most often , if not always , entangle with many inconvenience ; as first , for that the outrage during the vacancy , be many and great ; every one that be either grieve or in want , assume free power both for revenge and spoil . second , for that the bold win the garland more often than the best ; because the favour of the people do always taste more of affection then of judgement . third , for that they who do not leave their state to their posterity , will dissipate the domain and work out of it either profit or friend ; for so we see that the empire of Germany be pluck bare of her fair feather . four , for that occasion of war be hereby minister ; and that , either when one take his repulse for indignity ; upon which ground Francis the first , king of Fraūce , can never be drive out of practice against Charles the 5. emperor ; Ans. the part of the catechism be these two general , doctrine and discipline , and under doctrine these four contain . to wit the law of God , a sum whereof be in the ten commandment . faith , a sum whereof be in the 12. article . prayer , a form whereof be the lord prayer . the sacrament . of these part in order afterward : now to some question in your book . and first , que. what be the chief thing which every one ought to be most careful of , so long as they live ? An. every one ought to be most careful of these two point , first and chief how to be save in the day of judgement , before god judgement seat , and so to come to life everlasting . second how to live accord to God holy will during our life , in which two point whole stand the glory of God , so much as of man ought to be seek for . que. how may the first be prove ? the first reason draw of the vanity of all thing . Ans. the great vanity of all earthly thing , which man so hunt after , do show , that our care shall not be so much of these matter , as it be , but rather how to have our soul save , when all these shall have their end . for consider even the chief desire of man several , and see how in the attain of they there be no stay comfort , and yet often do man fail after much endeavour , and not attain to they . que. what chief desire of man can you name unto i ? the four commandment . remember that thou keep holy the sabaoth day . question . what be the meaning of this commandment ? Ans. your book answer that the hallow of the sabaoth day , be to rest from our labour in our call , and in one place to assemble our self together , and with fear and reverence to hear , mark and lay up in our heart the word of God preach unto we , to pray altogether that which we understand with one consent , and at the time appoint to use the sacrament in faith and repentance , and all our life long to rest from wickedness , that the lord by his holy spirit may work in we his good work , and so begin in this life everlasting rest . que. have not the Jew diverse feast beside this sabaoth ? Ans. yes in deed . some of God immediate appoint , and some by themselves upon special occasion . by you lord they be tie to three solemn feast in the year , at which he will have all the male to appear before he . to wit , the feast of unleaven bread , that be easter , Leuit. 23. Exod. 23.15 . or the passover in remembrance how the angel pass over their house , when in one night he slay all the first bear in Egypt both of man and beast . the feast of the harvest of the first fruit of their labour which they have sow in the field , verse . 16. which be Whitsuntide or Pentecost , in remembrance that you law be give fifty day after their departure out of Egypt . and the feast of gather fruit in the end of the year , verse . 16. when they have cleanse the field . this be the feast of tabernacle , put they in mind that 40. year they dwell in tent and tabernacle in the wilderness . beside these they have the first day of the month , the 7. year , the jubilee , and such other . Ans. there be in this commandment touch the sabaoth ever something moral , and something ceremonial . whatsoever be moral remain still to we as much to be observe as ever of they : and whatsoever then be but ceremonial , that be now by Christ take away , and we free from it . as for example , to have one day in the seven to serve the lord general in , that be moral and remain still , bind we unto it , as also to rest that day from all labour , let the course of piety and sanctification that that day ought to be keep . but to have precise the Saturday , and to rest so straite from all labour as they do , that be but ceremonial and a shadow , and therefore now abrogate by the come of the body Christ . que. yea but even the day also now in our church be change from Saturday to Sunday . Ans. truth it be , and for that matter mark and consider in the scripture , that as Christ in his time , so after he his disciple in their time do bear a while with the infirmity of the Jew and teach on the sabaoth , and Paul haste to Jerusalem against the feast of Pentecost , yet so , act . 13. act . 20. that ever still they insinuate a freedom by Christ , act 2. and 5. etc and therefore daily also meet , etc but when as false teacher vehement urge a necessity thereof , to show the freedom by Christ from day and time , then in deed stand they against it , and change even the very day quite and clean . que. but do the apostle in deed themselves make this alteration of the day ? Cor. 16.2 . mark . 16.2 . 1. Ans. the end of the remain of a sabaoth yet still in the church of God , be chief three . first that we may have a kind of resemblance and form of our eternal rest from sin in the kingdom of God . second , that by this mean servant and cattle may be provide for against the cruel greediness and satiable covetousness of some master and owner . and last , that ecclesiastical discipline and some estate of a christian common wealth in perform to the lord joint together demand duty , may this way be establish . que. of every one of these i pray you several , master N. for more plainness , and first , what be that spiritual rest and sanctify of the sabaoth which lay before we a resemblance of eternal rest ? Ans. that be when rest from worldly business , and from our own work and study , we yield our self whole to God governance , that he may do his work in we , and when ( as the scripture term it ) we crucify our flesh , we bridle the froward desire and motion of the heart , restrain our own nature that we may obey the will of God . for thus do our sabaoth day here upon earth shall most apt express a figure and likeness of the eternal and most holy rest which we shall for ever enjoy in heaven . yet ever must we know that these thing be not to be do only on the sabaoth , but even all our life long , every day of every private man , and this one day be appoint chief above the rest , for our negligence and weakness sake only , without which such appointment it be great to be fear some will never do it . que. and be this spiritual rest from sin in deed necessary in every one that will as he be bind sanctify the sabaoth ? Ans. sure it be even so necessary , as that without this all our other rest , put on our better apparel , go to the church , hear service , etc be nothing , but even abhor of God and detest . this be a great matter if we have grace to consider of it , yet nothing but plain and open scripture . for what say the prophet Easie ? bless be the man that do this , Easie . 56.2 . and the son of man which lay hold on it , he that keep the sabaoth and pollute it not , and keep his hand from do any evil . see what it be to keep holy the sabaoth in the judgement of the prophet , even to keep our hand from do any evil , that be to cease from sin and our own way . again the same prophet in another place : Easie . 58.13 . if thou consecrate the sabaoth as glorious to the lord , and shall honour he , not do thou own way , nor seek thy own will , etc and in his first chapter also , Easie . 1.13.14 . i can suffer your new moon , nor sabaothe , nor solemn day ( it be iniquity ) nor solemn assembly , my soul hate they , etc and why so ? the reason follow in the next word ( for your hand be full of blood ) that be , you abstain not from sin , remain still in avarice , deceit , cruelty , extortion , and such like , which as long as you do , howsoever you seem to sanctify the sabaoth , i abhor you and your do . and then follow notable a description even of this spiritual rest : wash you , make you clean , take away the evil of your work from before my eye , cease to do evil , learn to do well , seek judgement , relieve the oppress , judge the fatherless , defend the widow , etc many other such place be there . que. now i pray you of the second end and use of the sabaoth a little also .
